cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Emotional Intimacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Janine repeatedly attempts to reconnect with Tudor emotionally, but his growing detachment and silence progressively isolate her, eroding the foundation of their marriage.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine seeks emotional reassurance and connection from Tudor through small gestures and questions.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is left in anxious isolation, unable to bridge the emotional gap as Tudor withdraws further into himself.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional chasm between Janine and Tudor remains unaddressed, foreshadowing the eventual collapse of their relationship.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Passive Worry to Active Intervention
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spurred by concern and external pressure, Janine evolves from silently observing Tudor's decline to taking decisive steps to bring in medical help, asserting agency in a crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine is emotionally rebuffed when expressing concern about Tudor's health.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She successfully arranges for a doctor to visit, transitioning into the role of an active caregiver and advocate.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tudor's resistance and the doctor's skepticism cast doubt on whether her intervention will be effective or welcomed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Trauma and Flight
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Confronted with Tudor's grotesque transformation, Janine shifts from caregiving to terror, culminating in her desperate escape and search for safety.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine attempts to understand and care for Tudor despite his disturbing behavior.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She flees in panic, abandoning the apartment and seeking refuge with Betts, marking a complete breakdown of safety and normalcy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her psychological stability and physical safety remain uncertain as the true threat is still not fully understood.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Illusion of Sanctuary
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Janine turns to Betts as a source of emotional refuge, but the sanctuary she seeks becomes the site of a new betrayal and violation, subverting trust and intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine finds comfort in the familiarity and warmth of Betts's presence after emotional strain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The relationship turns horrific as Betts forces a parasite into Janine, transforming intimacy into trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Betts's true nature and motives remain ambiguous, leaving Janine's trust and safety fundamentally compromised.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Transformation and Assimilation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being violated by the parasite, Janine undergoes a complete metamorphosis, emerging not as a victim but as a willing agent of the collective transformation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine is a traumatized individual, forcibly infected and seemingly victimized by the parasitic force.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is reborn in calm, radiant control, actively participating in the transmission of the parasite to others.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin, purpose, and ultimate reach of the transformation remain unknown, leaving her new existence enigmatic and potentially expansive.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Suspicion to Investigator
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: St. Luc evolves from a doctor under suspicion due to his connection with the murdered Dr. Hobbes into an active investigator uncovering a parasitic outbreak. His initial goal of clearing his name shifts into a dedicated pursuit of medical truth, driven by accumulating evidence linking patients to a shared pathogen.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: St. Luc is motivated by self-preservation and professional credibility, seeking to understand why Hobbes contacted him.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He initiates urgent collaboration with Rollo to investigate abdominal masses, fully embracing the role of an epidemiological investigator.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin of the parasite and its connection to Hobbes' experiments remain unconfirmed, leaving the full scope of the threat undefined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Tudor Case and the Erosion of Medical Norms
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: St. Luc's engagement with Janine Tudor's case marks the beginning of his deviation from standard medical practice, triggering a chain of decisions that pull him deeper into the mystery. What starts as a procedural anomaly becomes a gateway to uncovering a hidden epidemic, blurring the lines between clinical duty and personal obsession.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: St. Luc prioritizes Tudor based on her representative status, showing early favoritism without clear medical justification.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He abandons academic preparation to respond to Forsythe's attack, now fully immersed in field investigation linked to the same network of patients.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Tudor's condition remains medically unverified, and her potential role in the parasite's spread is still ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into the Parasite's World
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: St. Luc confronts escalating physical and existential threats as he investigates the parasite's remains, culminating in lethal self-defense and a violent rescue mission. His conflict shifts from intellectual inquiry to survival against infected individuals, marking his transformation from healer to combatant.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: St. Luc enters the incinerator to retrieve biological evidence, operating as a scientist in a controlled investigation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He kills multiple infected individuals and secures Forsythe's escape, now fully engaged in life-or-death combat against the parasite's hosts.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral and psychological consequences of killing in self-defense, and whether the infected retain any humanity, remain unprocessed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Loss and Transformation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: St. Luc's relationship with Forsythe evolves from professional collaboration to intimate dependence, only to collapse as she becomes infected. His emotional arc traces loyalty, protection, and ultimate abandonment, culminating in his own infection and loss of self.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: St. Luc maintains professional boundaries, delaying personal connection with Forsythe despite mutual attraction.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He witnesses Forsythe's full transformation and is himself infected, losing both her and his autonomy to the parasite.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether St. Luc retains any agency post-infection or is fully subsumed by the parasite is unknown, leaving his identity in limbo.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: St. Luc transitions from a solitary investigator operating in secrecy to a visible leader orchestrating a mass escape. His status evolves from suspect to protector to revolutionary figurehead, symbolizing resistance against the parasitic control of Starliner Towers.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: St. Luc acts alone, descending into the building's lower levels to intervene directly as communication fails.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He leads a convoy out of Starliner Towers, now at the forefront of a collective exodus.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term viability of the escape and St. Luc's role in the outside world remain uncertain, especially given his possible infection.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Physical and Psychological Assimilation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tudor's physical deterioration escalates from concealed symptoms to full-body parasitic transformation, marked by increasing denial, loss of autonomy, and violent psychological shifts as he becomes a host to an invasive organism.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tudor hides a painful lump and denies illness, maintaining emotional and physical control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tudor is unconscious, then violently awakens with delusional attachment to parasites, attacking anyone who threatens them.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Tudor retains any human consciousness or if he has become entirely subsumed by the parasitic force.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fracture of Marital Trust and Emotional Abandonment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tudor's emotional withdrawal and concealment of his condition erode Janine's trust, culminating in her flight from their home and emotional realignment with Betts as she can no longer endure his physical and psychological deterioration.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tudor is sullen and dismissive during a morning interaction, emotionally distancing himself from Janine.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Janine flees to Betts' apartment, fully disengaging from Tudor and symbolically ending their relationship.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The possibility of reconciliation or accountability is extinguished, leaving Janine's trauma and Tudor's isolation unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Hidden Life and Secret Access
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tudor's clandestine entry into Annabelle's apartment reveals a hidden dimension of his life, abruptly shattered by the discovery of her brutal murder, thrusting him into a traumatic confrontation with death and potential implication.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tudor premeditatedly enters Annabelle's apartment undetected, suggesting a secret relationship or mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He discovers her burned corpse and flees in horror, now burdened with traumatic knowledge and possible danger.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The nature of Tudor's connection to Annabelle and whether he will be implicated in the crime remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Medical Mystery and Institutional Scrutiny
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Tudor's condition worsens, medical professionals begin to investigate his case, shifting from personal concern to institutional scrutiny, with his file becoming central to a larger inquiry into a mysterious infestation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Janine considers secretly involving a doctor due to Tudor's refusal to seek help.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: St. Luc intends to examine Tudor but is diverted by an emergency, leaving Tudor's case pending in a growing medical investigation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tudor's condition remains unassessed, and the delay risks unchecked progression of the infestation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Parasitic Emergence and Contagion Spread
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The parasite within Tudor evolves from a hidden presence to an active, visible force—first expelled, then bonded with, and finally spreading beyond Tudor to infect others in the building.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tudor violently expels a parasite, acknowledging its physical reality.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The parasite escapes into the hallway and attacks another resident, marking the beginning of cross-apartment transmission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The method of parasite control, origin, and potential containment remain unknown as the threat escalates uncontrollably.
